narrative: OMX30 faces headwinds from strong continental selloff despite resilient US tech. DAX and FTSE declines (r=0.89, r=0.78) and VIX surge indicate risk-off sentiment overwhelming correlation support from USD/SEK strength (+0.41%). Technically, oversold stochastic (K=4.2) and weak ADX (13.2) create conflicting signals—while the index sits +5.5% above 200-MA (supportive), MACD below signal and RSI 45.72 suggest consolidation. US jobless claims at 14:30 CET and continued yield volatility (4.552%) will be key.
morningBrief: Gap down expected on open: European contagion (DAX -150bps, FTSE -189bps) outweighs US resilience. VIX surge to 21.3 triggers defensive rotation; watch sector rotation into banks on NIM benefit from yields, but real estate likely pressured. Key support at 3085 (lower Bollinger); resistance 3180 (50-MA). ADX weakness (13.2) means reduce position conviction by 30%—trade ranges tactically. Exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco) get modest help from USD/SEK +0.41%, but offset by industrial input-cost pressure (Brent -0.47%). US jobs data at 14:30 CET is the main catalyst; any softness extends the selloff.
drivers: European equity weakness (-1.50% DAX, -1.89% FTSE) with high correlation (r=0.89, r=0.78)
VIX spike +12.58% to 21.3 signals risk-off rotation
Weak ADX (13.2) reduces confidence; oversold stochastic (K=4.2) flags mean reversion risk
US 10Y yield +35bp compresses tech/defensive valuations despite NASDAQ +0.86%

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +35bp to 4.552% widens NIM expectations despite risk-off backdrop.
Brent oil -0.47% and broad European selloff pressure input costs; USD/SEK strength (+0.41%) provides only partial offset.
Yield spike (10Y +35bp) raises refinancing costs and increases cap-rate compression fears.
NASDAQ +0.86% is outweighed by yield compression (4.552%) and risk-off VIX spike; ADX weakness limits upside conviction.
Risk-off environment and VIX surge typically rotate flows to defensives; oversold stochastic (K=4.2) may trigger short-covering.
Copper -0.86%, gold -1.59%, and broad commodity weakness override typical commodity demand from risk-on sentiment.

watchList.reason: Industrials anchor (export sensitive); USD/SEK +0.41% supports pricing, but monitor Brent and European demand signals.
Banks benefit from NIM expansion (yields +35bp) but risk-off may depress equity demand; oversold stochastic suggests contrarian entry.
Defensive rotation play; VIX spike and risk-off should support relative outperformance vs. cyclicals.
EUR/SEK +0.64% increases sourcing costs (negative); weak consumer sentiment in risk-off environment.
Tech weakness offset by potential mean-reversion play if oversold stochastic triggers; monitor NASDAQ sentiment.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (psychological; ADX weakness argues for consolidation between 3085–3150)
keyLevels.200DayMA: 3122 (index +5.5% above, bullish bias but weak conviction)

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (consensus +245k, prior +229k). No major Swedish data. ECB speakers: monitor for rate-cut signals given eurozone weakness.
topRisk: 1) Eurozone contagion deepens (DAX -150bps suggests demand shock); 2) US jobs data misses expectations, triggering 10Y yield spike and broader equity selloff; 3) ADX weakness allows gap-fill trades to whipsaw retail; 4) Yield compression on tech limits any NASDAQ-driven OMX30 bounce.
technicalSummary: Above 200-MA (+5.5%) but weakening momentum (MACD below signal, hist=-6.677)
RSI 45.72 neutral; no overbought/oversold mean-reversion flags yet, but stochastic K=4.2 is deeply oversold
ADX 13.2 (WEAK): reduce magnitude conviction by 30%; Bollinger %B=0.27 (lower half) confirms downside bias
52W range 81%: index near upper-middle; -4.8% from high suggests room for reversion
